content_markdown: Progressive (travelling) waves $S$ and $T$ have the same frequency and are in the same medium. $S$ has amplitude $2.0\text{ m}$ and $T$ has amplitude $4.0\text{ m}$. What is the ratio of the intensity of $T$ to the intensity of $S$?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: The intensity $I$ of a wave is proportional to the square of its amplitude $A$ ($I \propto A^2$). To find the ratio of the intensity of $T$ to $S$, we calculate $\frac{I_T}{I_S} = \frac{(A_T)^2}{(A_S)^2} = \frac{(4.0)^2}{(2.0)^2} = \frac{16}{4} = 4$.
